perturbation.original_text: 4.3 NTC shall invoice ALFA AESAR for payment of Products delivered to ALFA AESAR on a Net 30 day basis. Excepting any amounts disputed in good faith, balances unpaid after such period will be subject to a finance charge of 1% per month or may be offset against any balances owed by NTC to ALFA AESAR. The parties agree to work in good faith to resolve any disputed invoice. In the event such efforts do not resolve the dispute within sixty (60) days, either party may initiate Arbitration proceedings pursuant to Section 16.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.3 NTC shall invoice ALFA AESAR for payment of Products delivered to ALFA AESAR on a Net 30 day basis. Excepting any amounts disputed in good faith, balances unpaid after such period will be subject to a finance charge of 1% per month or may be offset against any balances owed by NTC to ALFA AESAR. However, if ALFA AESAR is not satisfied with the products after the 30-day window, ALFA AESAR reserves the right to withhold payments without incurring any additional penalties, interest, or finance charges. The parties agree to work in good faith to resolve any disputed invoice. In the event such efforts do not resolve the dispute within sixty (60) days, either party may initiate Arbitration proceedings pursuant to Section 16.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that balances unpaid after 30 days are subject to a finance charge of 1% per month, while simultaneously giving ALFA AESAR the right to withhold payment without penalty if they are not satisfied with the products, even after the 30-day window. This creates uncertainty regarding when finance charges apply, since Section 4.3 previously stated any invoices unpaid after 30 days are subject to fees. The added clause allows ALFA AESAR to bypass this requirement, contradicting the initial payment terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.3 NTC shall invoice ALFA AESAR for payment of Products delivered to ALFA AESAR on a Net 30 day basis. Excepting any amounts disputed in good faith, balances unpaid after such period will be subject to a finance charge of 1% per month or may be offset against any balances owed by NTC to ALFA AESAR. However, if ALFA AESAR is not satisfied with the products after the 30-day window, ALFA AESAR reserves the right to withhold payments without incurring any additional penalties, interest, or finance charges. The parties agree to work in good faith to resolve any disputed invoice. In the event such efforts do not resolve the dispute within sixty (60) days, either party may initiate Arbitration proceedings pursuant to Section 16.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that unpaid balances after 30 days are subject to a 1% monthly finance charge. The added clause in the changed text allows ALFA AESAR to withhold payments without penalty if they are not satisfied with the products, even after the 30-day window. This directly conflicts with the original statement about finance charges applying to all unpaid balances after 30 days. This clearly falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations or penalties.

perturbation.original_text:
5.  Returns; Discontinued Products.
5.1 Return Rights. Subject to the protocol described below, ALFA AESAR shall have the right to return at NTC's expense, and for full credit of ALFA AESAR's cost, any Products (i.) for which a legitimate and credible allegation is made that the use of such Products infringes on any patent, trademark, trade secret, copyright, right of privacy or publicity, or any other tangible or 3 intangible proprietary or intellectual property right; (b) that are not manufactured, packaged, or labeled in accordance with specifications or industry standards; (c) that are shipped in error or in non-conformance with ALFA AESAR's purchase order; or (d) that are damaged or defective. In the event that ALFA AESAR believes that it is entitled to return any Products delivered under this Agreement, the parties will adhere to the following protocol: (w) ALFA AESAR will contact NTC's Quality Director and then forward to the Quality Director, via a carrier selected by and at NTC's expense, a sample of the Product that ALFA AESAR believes is non-conforming; (x) upon receipt of the sample, NTC will test it and then notify ALFA AESAR of the test results; (y) where the test results confirm that the sample is non-conforming, NTC will provide ALFA AESAR with NTC's Return Authorization Number ("RAN"); and (z) any returned non-conforming Product must be in its original container, with original labels in act, and all paperwork concerning the returned Product must include NTC's RAN.
perturbation.changed_text:
5.  Returns; Discontinued Products.
5.1 Return Rights. Subject to the protocol described below, ALFA AESAR shall have the right to return at NTC's expense, and for full credit of ALFA AESAR's cost, any Products (i.) for which a legitimate and credible allegation is made that the use of such Products infringes on any patent, trademark, trade secret, copyright, right of privacy or publicity, or any other tangible or 3 intangible proprietary or intellectual property right; (b) that are not manufactured, packaged, or labeled in accordance with specifications or industry standards; (c) that are shipped in error or in non-conformance with ALFA AESAR's purchase order; or (d) that are damaged or defective. Notwithstanding the above, ALFA AESAR may return any product, at any time, for any reason, and receive a full refund. In the event that ALFA AESAR believes that it is entitled to return any Products delivered under this Agreement, the parties will adhere to the following protocol: (w) ALFA AESAR will contact NTC's Quality Director and then forward to the Quality Director, via a carrier selected by and at NTC's expense, a sample of the Product that ALFA AESAR believes is non-conforming; (x) upon receipt of the sample, NTC will test it and then notify ALFA AESAR of the test results; (y) where the test results confirm that the sample is non-conforming, NTC will provide ALFA AESAR with NTC's Return Authorization Number ("RAN"); and (z) any returned non-conforming Product must be in its original container, with original labels in act, and all paperwork concerning the returned Product must include NTC's RAN.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence, "Notwithstanding the above, ALFA AESAR may return any product, at any time, for any reason, and receive a full refund," directly contradicts the established return protocol. Previously, ALFA AESAR could only return products under specific conditions (infringement, manufacturing defects, shipping errors, etc.) and had to follow a detailed process. The new sentence allows returns for *any* reason, *at any time*, undermining the purpose of the original return protocol. While the text still mentions the return authorization number protocol, it creates uncertainty regarding the validity of the initial return protocol because ALFA AESAR can bypass this entirely.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 Return Rights. Subject to the protocol described below, ALFA AESAR shall have the right to return at NTC's expense, and for full credit of ALFA AESAR's cost, any Products (i.) for which a legitimate and credible allegation is made that the use of such Products infringes on any patent, trademark, trade secret, copyright, right of privacy or publicity, or any other tangible or 3 intangible proprietary or intellectual property right; (b) that are not manufactured, packaged, or labeled in accordance with specifications or industry standards; (c) that are shipped in error or in non-conformance with ALFA AESAR's purchase order; or (d) that are damaged or defective. Notwithstanding the above, ALFA AESAR may return any product, at any time, for any reason, and receive a full refund. In the event that ALFA AESAR believes that it is entitled to return any Products delivered under this Agreement, the parties will adhere to the following protocol: (w) ALFA AESAR will contact NTC's Quality Director and then forward to the Quality Director, via a carrier selected by and at NTC's expense, a sample of the Product that ALFA AESAR believes is non-conforming; (x) upon receipt of the sample, NTC will test it and then notify ALFA AESAR of the test results; (y) where the test results confirm that the sample is non-conforming, NTC will provide ALFA AESAR with NTC's Return Authorization Number ("RAN"); and (z) any returned non-conforming Product must be in its original container, with original labels in act, and all paperwork concerning the returned Product must include NTC's RAN.
perturbation.justification: The added sentence in the changed text, "Notwithstanding the above, ALFA AESAR may return any product, at any time, for any reason, and receive a full refund," directly contradicts the initial part of Section 5.1, which specifies conditions under which ALFA AESAR can return products (infringement, defects, etc.) and outlines a detailed return protocol. The new sentence allows returns for any reason and at any time, undermining the purpose and enforceability of the existing return protocol and the conditions specified in the original text. The contradicted text and Changed Text are in the same location, making the contradiction self-evident.
